behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff plus HTTP mapping: bad signature must both produce 401 and suppress enqueue. MOCK-MISS: this is not just HMAC math; it is cross-module control flow. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejected higher levels because behavior text has no real network/OS/subprocess requirement and no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with a deterministic invalid signature and a spy/fake queue writer. Assert status=401 and queue write was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering across modules (HTTP handler and queue persistence API). MOCK-MISS: unit test of one function would miss async ordering defects at boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; in-process queue adapter/fake persistent layer can expose call order. No AI/multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process server/handler with a queue implementation that records write completion timestamp and gate the write promise. Verify 200 is emitted only after write resolves.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Boundary is enqueue/dispatch vs downstream processor lifecycle. Need to verify response decouples from processor completion. REAL-INFRA not required: controlled in-process processor promise reproduces the failure. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/subprocess timing, only non-blocking orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a downstream processor that blocks on an unresolved promise, send a valid event, and assert HTTP response returns while processor is still pending.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA boundary: wall-clock latency under concurrency depends on real HTTP server/runtime scheduling/event-loop behavior; in-process fakes/timer mocks can hide contention and backpressure effects (MOCK-HIDE). No AI dependency. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires wall-clock SLA under concurrent slow work.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service in a real Node process, generate concurrent slow downstream load from earlier events, then send a new webhook over real HTTP and assert end-to-end response latency <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is dedupe state handoff between handler and persistent queue/repository. Unit logic alone can miss real duplicate checks tied to persisted state/unique constraints. REAL-INFRA: local persistence is sufficient; no OS/network/subprocess requirement. No AI/multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real local persistence-backed queue module in test mode, submit the same event ID twice through the handler, assert first write succeeds and second does not create a new queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Cross-module contract: queue-full signal/error must map to specific HTTP status/header. MOCK-MISS: pure unit of formatter misses real mapping path through handler. REAL-INFRA not needed; an in-process queue stub that emits capacity error is enough. Rejected System because no real OS/network behavior is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler in-process with queue write mocked to return a typed 'queue full' error; assert response is 503 and includes Retry-After.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets the queue component plus its persistence/config wiring, not just a pure algorithm. Need real local storage path/module interaction to catch enforcement bugs. REAL-INFRA does not require external network/subprocess; local DB/filesystem integration matches level definition. No AI/multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against a local test datastore/filesystem with capacity N, enqueue N events, then assert enqueue N+1 is rejected with the expected capacity error.
